reasoning: The LLM fidelity analysis found only one minor formatting defect and suggested AUTO_APPROVE. However, programmatic detectors identified a critical defect: headers and footers from the source PDF were retained across all 5 pages in the extraction, creating significant accessibility issues for screen readers. Additionally, a major defect regarding flattened sub-lists was detected. According to the count-based routing rules, the presence of any critical defect requires an immediate REJECT.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (5): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 5; Page 3 of 5
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=5, pages_affected=5/5. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (1 list(s)): p1:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=1. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

The source PDF contains underlined headings for every bullet point in the 'March 1, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary' and 'Written Public Comment Summary' lists (e.g., 'Beneficial Reuse Areas', 'Costs', 'Dam Safety'). The extraction completely loses this underline emphasis, presenting the text plainly without any markdown formatting.

action_items: Strip all duplicate header/footer artifacts from the rendered HTML
Correct list nesting levels to properly distinguish sub-points from main list items
Restore missing underline formatting for bullet point headings
